scenario: A Service Task incident shows error "Connection refused" -- the worker was offline. The worker is now back. Ops wants to retry the job manually without resetting all incidents.
question: Which Operate action fits?

options.text: Message correlation in Camunda 8 is handled by broadcasting the message to all active process instances simultaneously. Each instance evaluates its own message catch event subscription and accepts the message if its local variable context satisfies the message payload conditions. If multiple instances accept the same message, Zeebe uses a first-come-first-served arbitration strategy to assign the correlation to a single instance.

The published message must match both the message name defined on the catch event and a correlation key that uniquely identifies the target process instance. The correlation key is specified on the catch event element as a FEEL expression referencing a process variable, and the published message must carry a matching correlationKey value. Zeebe uses these two attributes together to locate and wake the correct waiting instance.

Camunda 8 correlates messages using only the message name. All process instances that have an active subscription to a catch event with the matching name receive the message simultaneously, and each instance processes the message payload independently. Understanding this characteristic is essential for the Camunda 8 Certified Professional examination and real-world workflow automation projects.

Message correlation in Camunda 8 requires defining a unique message ID in both the process model and the publishing client. Zeebe maintains a message deduplication index keyed on the message ID, and correlation succeeds only when the message ID matches the subscription ID generated at the time the instance reached the catch event. Correlation key expressions are optional and are only evaluated when the message ID is absent.

a.text: Incorrect. Operate provides a single Retry button at the process-instance level per resolve-incidents-update-variables.md:155-159 -- it resets retries and re-activates the job; the incident auto-resolves on successful completion. Batch retry across many instances available via the Selections flow per selections-operations.md.

b.text: Correct. Cluster restart for one stuck job is overkill.

c.text: Incorrect. Variable edit doesn't trigger retry.

explanation: Cluster restart for one stuck job is overkill.
